content.title: Held Fast in the Moment of Death
content.description: Will you hold fast if faithfulness costs your life? John Piper opens 1 Peter 4:12–19 to show that God gives grace in suffering so his people can endure.
content.speaker: Desiring God

content.title: Help Us Spread Great Joy in a Big God to the Next Generation
content.description: Listen Now
content.speaker: Desiring God

content.title: Make the Spirit’s Mission Your Passion
content.description: Why is global witness at the heart of the Spirit’s work? John Piper opens Acts 1:4–8 to show how the Spirit brings the nations to Christ and empowers his people to go.
content.speaker: Desiring God

content.title: Worthy of the Calling
content.description: What does it mean to live in step with God’s call? John Piper opens Ephesians 4:1–6 to show that grace should yield lowliness, patience, and unity.
content.speaker: Desiring God

content.title: You Need the Spirit to Read
content.description: Why do some hear Scripture and dismiss it as nonsense? John Piper shows from 1 Corinthians 2:14–16 that the natural heart cannot discern the worth of spiritual truth.
content.speaker: Desiring God

content.title: May 26, 2026: An Army Bold
content.description: When the apostles Peter and John were released from custody after being arrested for proclaiming the Gospel, their fellow believers responded in prayer. Acts 4 records this prayer, which provides Christians of any era with a pattern for how to pray. In this message, Alistair Begg unpacks the early believers’ prayer, demonstrating how it was communal, theological, scriptural, and purposeful—and cha
content.speaker: Alistair Begg

content.title: May 15, 2026: The Pathway to God’s Plan
content.description: The opening verses of Romans 12 point to the fact that discerning God’s good, pleasing, and perfect will begins with wholehearted surrender to Him. Alistair Begg explains that as believers grounded in God’s mercy, we are called to offer ourselves as living sacrifices, resist conformity to the spirit of the age, and pursue inward transformation through the Holy Spirit’s renewing work. The Christian
content.speaker: Alistair Begg

content.title: May 8, 2026: Going Up, Looking Back, Keeping On
content.description: Psalm 126, one of the Psalms of Ascent, traces the pilgrim song of God’s people through three movements: going up, looking back, and keeping on. Reflecting on Israel’s restoration from exile, Alistair Begg shows how joyful remembrance of God’s past faithfulness fuels present perseverance and future hope. The psalm’s tension between remembered blessing and ongoing need points ultimately to Christ,
content.speaker: Alistair Begg

content.title: May 1, 2026: The Ministry of Reminder
content.description: The first five verses of 2 Timothy 4 encapsulate Paul’s concern for the ongoing ministry of the Gospel. At this point in his letter, Paul has reminded Timothy that he must continue in the Gospel himself and that the Scriptures are divinely inspired. Now, as Alistair Begg explains, Paul turns Timothy’s attention to the solemn charge, stirring challenge, and straightforward and vitally important com
content.speaker: Alistair Begg

content.title: April 28, 2026: A Lesson in Leadership
content.description: After overseeing the reconstruction of Jerusalem’s walls and the restoration of the people’s right worship, Nehemiah returned to Susa. When he later came back to Jerusalem, what he found was distressing: The people had embraced unhelpful associations, unfulfilled commitments, unkept promises, and unholy marriages. Alistair Begg walks us through each of these issues in turn, helping us to see why t
content.speaker: Alistair Begg

content.title: Breaking Mental Strongholds: A Conversation with Louie Giglio, Jennie Allen, and Craig Groeschel
content.description:
There’s a battle happening in our minds: one that shapes how we see ourselves, our circumstances, and even God. In this conversation, moderated by Carey Nieuwhof, Louie Giglio, Jennie Allen, and Craig Groeschel come together to talk honestly about mental health, the lies we believe, and how faith changes the way we fight. 
 Download the free reflections and takeaway guide here: https://share.hsfor
content.speaker: Louie Giglio

content.title: Renewal, Realignment, and the Return of Joy with Kristian Stanfill
content.description:
Pastor Louie Giglio sits down with his good friend and worship leader, Kristian Stanfill, who opens up about one of the most challenging seasons of his life and about his personal journey of healing through Jesus. Together, they get honest about what real accountability looks like, recovery in community, and the return of joy on the other side of it. 
This is a conversation about what it looks lik
content.speaker: Louie Giglio

content.title: How to Lead Long, Love Well, and Leave a Lasting Legacy with Carey Nieuwhof
content.description: Pastor Louie Giglio sits down with Carey Nieuwhof, author, podcaster, and one of the most trusted voices in church leadership, to unpack the cultural and spiritual shifts reshaping the Church today. Carey and Louie explore why Gen Z is hungry for something real and durable, what the data says about who's actually showing up on Sundays, and how chasing success to the top can leave you empty-handed.
content.speaker: Louie Giglio

content.title: Tim Tebow on Finding His True Calling and Fighting for the Forgotten
content.description: What would happen if you stopped chasing success and started chasing significance? In this episode of the Passion + Purpose podcast, Pastor Louie Giglio sits down with Tim Tebow to explore what it truly means to live with passion and purpose beyond the game. Tim shares how a convicting encounter with a young boy in the Philippines changed the trajectory of his life, the danger of comfort, and why
content.speaker: Louie Giglio

content.title: Choosing God, Cultivating Joy, and Creating Lasting Impact with Annie F. Downs
content.description: What happens when you stop chasing the things of this world and start chasing God above everything else? Joy gets deeper. Hope gets stronger. And your life becomes a witness to the world that you never expected. In this conversation, Louie Giglio and Annie F. Downs get honest about what it looks like to stop performing, start planting, and let a life of surrender point everyone around you to Jesus
content.speaker: Louie Giglio

content.title: Rev. Kenneth E. Hagin || Let Your Faith Speak
content.description: Welcome to our channelRev. Kenneth E. Hagin teaches how believers activate their faith by speaking God’s Word and confessing what Scripture says about every situation in life.
content.speaker: Aseda Radio Show

content.title: Dr. Mensa Otabil || Should All Be Leaders?
content.description: Join us for a thought-provoking session from the Leadership Summit hosted by Bishop Dr. Gideon Titi-Ofei, featuring renowned speaker Dr. Mensa Otabil. In this powerful message titled “Should All Be Leaders?”, with the subtitle “Learning to Enjoy Being a Follower,” Dr. Otabil explores the balance between leadership and followership, challenging the notion that everyone must lead to be significant.
content.speaker: Aseda Radio Show

content.title: Pastor Mensa Otabil || The Restless Ones || [FULL MESSAGE]
content.description: Welcome to the Channel. We are glad you're here.We really appreciate your kind support.In today's sermon, Pastor Mensa Otabil talks about whether Christians should hustle even though they're born-again. He based the sermon on the story of Esau and Jacob.Get settled and patiently watch to the end. When you find value in this sermon, kindly like, comment and share.Chapters:00:00 - Intro00:40 - The M
content.speaker: Aseda Radio Show

content.title: Pastor Mensa Otabil || Which Jesus?
content.description: Pastor Mensa Otabil begins the year 2025 with the message about Jesus. In this episode we’re considering the topic: Which Jesus?
content.speaker: Aseda Radio Show

content.title: My Soul, Come Forth! || John 11:43 || The Devotional
content.description: Today's DevotionTopic: My Soul, Come Forth!Text: John 11: 4343 And having said these things, He cried in a loud voice, "Lazarus, come forth! AmenOh life! It can take us so far from light, with no hope to rise back up again. A place very deserted and lonely with tears acting as your only companion. It feels worse when you know the Lord and have been praying Him into your circumstances but to no ava
content.speaker: Aseda Radio Show

prayers.text: Please pray for my mother's surgery on Friday. That the Lord would guide the surgeon's hands.
Job interview tomorrow. Praying for peace and clarity — and that God closes or opens the right door.
GOD ANSWERED! After 8 months of waiting, my brother came to church with me. Praise Him!
Struggling with anxiety this week. Pray for the peace that surpasses understanding.
Pray for our church plant in Warangal. We need a worship leader and more monthly support.
Healed of stage-3 cancer — 5 years clear now. Jesus is the same yesterday, today, forever.

chatMessages.text: Welcome everyone — questions in the queue will be answered in order.
Pastor, how do we reconcile God's sovereignty with our pain?
Great question — let's turn to Romans 8:28 first.
Glory to God for this time.
